title: Audit-log continuity: detect indexing gap indicating lost evidence
exclusions: Does NOT detect confidentiality compromise of log content itself, nor physical tampering of the indexer host; see UC-22.35.2 (indexer integrity) and UC-22.35.3 (log-content confidentiality).
evidence: Saved search 'audit_log_gap_detection' (daily) outputs a per-source gap report to the compliance summary index; dashboard panel 'Audit log continuity — last 30d' on the evidence-pack dashboard.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Detects interruption of the audit trail (the 'ability to ensure the ongoing ... resilience of processing systems'), but the clause also requires confidentiality and integrity guarantees not covered here.
§164.312(b) 'Audit controls' is satisfied when the covered entity can show audit logs are captured continuously; this UC produces that evidence directly.

PCI 10.2.1 requires that audit logs are enabled and active for all system components; this UC detects loss of telemetry but does not validate 10.2.1's sub-requirements (10.2.1.1-10.2.1.7) for specific event types.
CC7.2 requires monitoring of system components 'to detect anomalies' — detecting audit-log gaps is one necessary signal but the criterion covers broader system monitoring.
A material weakness finding for ITGCs often follows from unexplained gaps in the audit trail; this UC raises the signal but SOX evidence requires additional management attestation.
Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.32(1)(b) (parent assurance='partial'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When any ingest source known to produce continuous events stops producing events for more than 60 seconds (and no planned-maintenance window is active), the UC fires within the next search interval and names the affected source.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Normal event-rate jitter (up to 30-second gaps), or a source in a pre-declared maintenance window (tag=maintenance), does NOT fire the UC.

dataSources: _internal metrics.log (group=per_sourcetype_thruput), _audit (action=indexing), or any index-time metric that exposes per-source event rates.
app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (native; no separate TA required)
spl:
| tstats count as events where index=_internal source=*metrics.log* group=per_sourcetype_thruput earliest=-30m by sourcetype _time span=1m
| eventstats avg(events) as avg_events, stdev(events) as stdev_events by sourcetype
| where events=0 AND avg_events>0
| eval gap_seconds=60
| table _time sourcetype events avg_events stdev_events gap_seconds
| sort - _time
description: Detects any sourcetype that was previously producing events but has stopped indexing for more than a rolling 60-second window. A regression in event ingestion is the earliest indicator that the audit trail required by almost every modern regulation has been compromised — whether by accident (agent failure, network partition, licensing quota) or by intent (attacker clearing logs).
value: Without continuous audit logs, compliance claims are unfalsifiable. Auditors across GDPR, HIPAA, PCI DSS, SOC 2, and SOX all treat log-gaps as a finding. This UC lets a customer show the auditor a green dashboard: 'here is every second of the last 30 days, accounted for by source.'
implementation: (1) Schedule every 5 minutes; (2) route hits to a restricted summary index (compliance_summary); (3) wire to ITSI service health or ES notable depending on deployment; (4) maintain a maintenance-window lookup (maintenance_windows.csv) listing tagged sources that should NOT alert.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (native; no separate TA required).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: _internal metrics.log (group=per_sourcetype_thruput), _audit (action=indexing), or any index-time metric that exposes per-source event rates..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Schedule every 5 minutes; (2) route hits to a restricted summary index (compliance_summary); (3) wire to ITSI service health or ES notable depending on deployment; (4) maintain a maintenance-window lookup (maintenance_windows.csv).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| tstats count as events where index=_internal source=*metrics.log* group=per_sourcetype_thruput earliest=-30m by sourcetype _time span=1m
| eventstats avg(events) as avg_events, stdev(events) as stdev_events by sourcetype
| where events=0 AND avg_events>0
| eval gap_seconds=60
| table _time sourcetype events avg_events stdev_events gap_seconds
| sort - _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Audit-log continuity: detect indexing gap indicating lost evidence** — Without continuous audit logs, compliance claims are unfalsifiable. Auditors across GDPR, HIPAA, PCI DSS, SOC 2, and SOX all treat log-gaps as a finding.

Documented **Data sources**: _internal metrics.log (group=per_sourcetype_thruput), _audit (action=indexing), or any index-time metric that exposes per-source event rates. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (native; no separate TA required). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _internal.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against precomputed summaries; ensure the referenced data model is accelerated.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sourcetype** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where events=0 AND avg_events>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap_seconds** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Audit-log continuity: detect indexing gap indicating lost evidence**): table _time sourcetype events avg_events stdev_events gap_seconds
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline of event rate per sourcetype with red bars for gap periods; heat-map of sources-by-day with green/amber/red cells; single value for '% of last 30d with full coverage'.
visualization: Timeline of event rate per sourcetype with red bars for gap periods; heat-map of sources-by-day with green/amber/red cells; single value for '% of last 30d with full coverage'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1562.008 Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs

knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance windows or expected cold-standby sources that receive low volume. Mitigate with a maintenance_windows.csv lookup and a `not source IN [$lookup]` clause.
